questions.text: Which enterprise system acts as an organization-wide application that unifies and integrates diverse business functions into a single system?
questions.hint: It is often considered the crown jewel of system integration.

questions.text: When a system is described as 'heterogeneous' in the context of System Integration and Architecture, what does it mean?
options: It consists of components that are identical in nature.
It is diverse in content, character, platform, or language.
It runs exclusively on a single local server.
It does not require any form of network connectivity.
questions.hint: Think about systems running on entirely different operating systems or platforms needing to speak to each other.

questions.text: Which organizational benefit of an Information System enables management to analyze historical impacts and prepare accurate cost estimates or forecasts?
questions.hint: This benefit removes the heavy time penalties associated with manual record searches.

questions.text: In the horizontal view of an enterprise, which of the following represents a collection of independent functional areas often referred to as 'silos'?
options: Accounting, Marketing, Human Resources, and Operations
questions.hint: These represent cross-departmental divisions executing primary and secondary activities.

questions.text: What vertical level of management handles long-term vision, competitive positioning, and enterprise strategy?
questions.hint: This is the executive light guiding the organization's overarching destination.

questions.text: Complete the statement regarding real-world business realities: 'No matter how well you create an IoT system, a parking counter, or a game, it cannot succeed if it doesn't...'
questions.hint: Technical excellence alone does not guarantee success without client validation and demand alignment.

questions.text: Systems integration only addresses pure technical software engineering and has no influence on employee motivation or internal communication paths.
questions.hint: Recall the 'Behavioral Change' benefit where transparent fluid information flows motivate lower levels.

questions.text: System Integration creates a symbiosis that allows scattered subsystems to function together as a cohesive whole to achieve overarching enterprise functionality.
questions.hint: Think about how individual components combine into an all-encompassing system.

questions.text: Which core benefit of SIA focuses on the fluid movement of information upward and downward, thereby increasing engagement and transparency?
questions.hint: It directly impacts how employers and employees interact and stay motivated.

questions.text: Which architectural component provides connectivity to suppliers, customers, and shareholders while displaying real-time visibility?
questions.hint: It links internal subsystems to the broader external commercial environment.

questions.text: What is defined as the operational model that determines how a company communicates internally and handles external client interactions?
questions.hint: Before deploying ice cream in a hot climate, you must understand the target environment.

questions.text: In the context of the Integration Lifecycle, which phase focuses heavily on listing detailed custom operational specifications directly provided by the client?
questions.hint: It is the preliminary baseline phase where all parameters are itemized.

questions.text: Which phase of the System Integration Lifecycle entails long-term monitoring, maintenance routines, updates, and active system modifications?
questions.hint: This phase checks the system's ongoing health after deployment.

questions.text: Look at the hierarchy of Information Systems. Which system sits at the lowest foundation layer, managing raw daily store-keeping data, payroll, and stock control tools?
questions.hint: It operates in either Batch Processing or Online Transaction modes.

questions.text: Which Information System category extracts raw data from a TPS and converts it into structured, summarized clean reports targeted at mid-level management?
questions.hint: Examples include sales management metrics and administrative operational reports.

questions.text: Which AI-driven Information System embeds specialized human knowledge and tools to assist professionals in parsing complex diagnostic scenarios?
questions.hint: It relies on a dedicated knowledge base and an inference engine.

questions.text: Review the Python program execution structure in the Skill-Building activity (`sia1.py`). What is the explicit declared name of the class object?
questions.hint: It houses the `__init__` constructor and the primary operational loop.

questions.text: In the `sia1.py` source script, what specific identifier is instantiated in the execution step to execute the application workflow loop?
questions.hint: Look closely at the script's entry method logic: `___ = cashieringsystem()`.

questions.text: According to Module 2 foundations, a 'System' refers to the individual pieces with a variety of distinct behaviors which, when assembled together along functional lines, creates a unified framework.
questions.hint: Assembling heterogeneous components simplifies a complicated operational environment.

questions.text: Decision Support Systems (DSS) are static platforms that process rigid daily store transactions and are completely incapable of running predictive data model scenarios.
questions.hint: Recall that a DSS provides interactive toolsets specifically to help parse unstructured choices.
